name: Potpie
description: codebase knowledge-graph platform providing structural context and agents over a repo

developer-org.comment: Startup founded by Aditi Kothari and Dhiren Mathur; raised a $2.2M pre-seed round.

first-release.comment: Founders describe launching Potpie publicly in January 2025 after ~2 years of development; exact repo-open date not pinned in primary docs.

form-factor.comment: v2.0 is CLI-first and also exposes an MCP server and HTTP surface; a 'Potpie for VS Code' extension and an account-backed managed/cloud offering exist.

standalone-usable.comment: Standalone CLI/MCP server that plugs into external agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode); not welded into one host.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool, not an engine bundled inside a single host agent.

install-mechanism.comment: v2.0 CLI installs via pip / uv tool install; self-host has historically used Docker; managed cloud is hosted.

retrieval-technique.comment: Parses each file into an AST, matches against the language grammar, and stores symbol definitions as nodes and references as edges in a knowledge graph. Secondary write-ups also describe an embedding-based fuzzy node lookup over the graph, but this is not confirmed in the primary docs fetched.

query-interface.comment: Agents call typed tools (resolve, search, record, status) against the context engine; humans ask natural-language questions; symbol/node resolution is a core operation.

returns.comment: Returns code for graph nodes, symbols and their relationships/call chains, and agent-generated summaries/answers over the graph.

structural-awareness.comment: AST parsing with a symbol/reference graph — genuinely structure-aware.

chunking-strategy.comment: Graph nodes are symbol definitions (functions, classes) extracted from the AST rather than fixed-size windows.

reranking.comment: No documented second-stage reranker (cross-encoder / LLM judge / graph PageRank) found in the primary docs fetched; multi-hop graph traversal is used but not described as a rerank stage.

index-location.comment: Graph persists locally for the self-hosted CLI/daemon; a managed cloud offering also hosts the index.

incremental-updates.comment: Graph is keyed per commit; repeat queries for the same commit skip re-parsing, and a change-detection capability targets changed code.

real-time-watch.comment: Parsing is triggered on request / per commit rather than by an automatic file-change watcher in the docs fetched.

vector-store.comment: Primary docs fetched describe a graph store (nodes/edges) but do not name a vector store; secondary write-ups mention embedding fuzzy lookup and Neo4j graph storage, unconfirmed here.

embedding-model.comment: No default embedding model named in the primary docs fetched; LLMs are model-agnostic via LiteLLM but the embedding model is undisclosed.

mcp-support.comment: Docs state users and agents interact via 'the CLI, MCP server, or HTTP surface'.

agent-compatibility.comment: Integrates with Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, and OpenCode (other); the MCP server works with any MCP-capable client.

ide-integrations.comment: 'Potpie for VS Code' extension, CLI in the terminal, and a managed web UI (potpie ui / login).

language-agnostic.comment: Marketing describes it as 'language agnostic', but structural parsing matches files against per-language grammars, so retrieval quality depends on grammar/parser support.

supported-languages.value: Parses via per-language grammars; Python and JavaScript/TypeScript among supported languages — exact set not enumerated in the docs fetched
supported-languages.comment: Docs describe grammar-based symbol extraction without publishing a definitive language list.

code-leaves-machine.comment: In the default managed/cloud configuration code is uploaded and sent to LLM providers; a self-hosted deployment with local models can keep code on-prem.

local-only-option.comment: Self-hostable and model-agnostic via LiteLLM (e.g. local ollama models), enabling an offline path; full offline still depends on a local embedding provider.

self-hostable.comment: Apache-2.0 OSS with documented self-hosting; enterprise offers on-prem deployment.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry posture not documented in the primary sources fetched.

max-repo-scale.comment: Positioned for large codebases; independent write-ups suggest it works well up to roughly single-language repos of ~500k LOC (secondary, not confirmed in primary docs).

pricing-model.comment: OSS/self-host is free; managed Pro has a free tier (50 requests/mo, or unlimited with your own API keys — BYOK); Enterprise is custom-priced.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free OSS/self-host and a free managed Pro tier; only Enterprise sits above it at custom (non-published) pricing.

maturity.comment: Funded, ~5.5k stars and 22 releases, but a recent v2.0 re-architecture (July 2026) means the surface is still evolving.

community-size.comment: ~5.5k GitHub stars and a funded startup community.
